text: Firefighters making progress on the more than 600 wildfires burning across the state. Let's start with the SCU Lightning Complex fire. The fire has burned more than 363,000 acres in Stanislaus, San Joaquin, Contra Costa, Alameda, and Santa Clara counties. It has become the second largest fire in state history. The fire is now 15 percent contained. Flames destroyed 18 buildings. Cal Fire has not said if any of those are homes. The LNU Lightning Complex slipped into the third largest fire in state history, burning nearly 353,000 acres in Solano, Yolo, Lake, Napa, and Sonoma counties. It is 27 percent contained. More than 900 structures have burned, including dozens of homes in Vacaville. Five people have died in the fires, including two in Solano County. In Butte County, a complex of fires that also includes Tehama and Glenn counties has burned just under 50,000 acres. It is 13 percent contained. Meanwhile, the Potter's Fire near Lake Oroville remains at 930 acres and 20 percent containment. People evacuated from the Mock Fire in Tulare County can now go back home. The 2,800 acre fires now 40 percent contained. Anyone living off of Highway 49 or Priest Colterville areas will need to show ID to get in because only residents will be allowed into that area.
